MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO STRIKE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES (TRANSACTION ID # 59982281) FILED BY DEFENDANT LIFEMARK GROUP INC. DBA SKYLAWN MEMORIAL PARK

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LIFEMARK GROUP INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT IS: A) SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW AS TO ALL SIGNATORY PLAINTIFFS AND B) OVERRULED AS TO ALL TEN CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO PLAINTIFFS KWAI HO TSANG AND LAI FUN TSANG, WHO ARE MINORS AND NOT SIGNATORIES. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT LIFEMARK GROUP INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYERS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, RESTITUTION AND DISGORGEMENT IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. AS STATED IN THE RULING ON LIFEMARK'S DEMURRER, PLAINTIFFS HAVE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED THEIR EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND THEIR TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UNFAIR COMPETITION LAW, WHICH SUPPORT THE PRAYERS THAT ARE SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN BY THIS MOTION. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-23-2017 Continued To Oct-18-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-30-2017 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: No appearances on WED-AUG-30-2017. The Court adopts the tentative rulings as follows:

Defendant Lifemark Group Inc.'s Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint

Defendant Lifemark Group Inc.'s demurrer to the sixth cause of action in plaintiffs' second amended complaint is overruled. Liberally construing the complaint, plaintiffs have adequately alleged a fraud claim based on a theory that defendant intentionally represented Spaces 3 and 4 at Skylawn Memorial Park would not be used by anyone other than plaintiffs, the representation was false given an alleged scheme to upsell customers, defendant knew the representation was false, and plaintiffs reasonably relied on the representation when deciding to purchase Spaces 3 and 4. Order signed in open court. Judge: Hon. Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: L. Fong; Proceedings not reported. (504)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Continued To Jul-26-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Lifemark Group Inc.'s demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for fraud and overruled as to the eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and the tenth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law. Plaintiffs have not adequately alleged reliance to support their fraud cause of action. Plaintiffs acknowledge that their fraud cause of action is not well pled and their request for leave to amend is granted. Paragraphs 49 and 50 of the FAC, incorporated by reference into the IIED cause of action, sufficiently allege outrageous conduct and intent to harm for a viable IIED claim. Paragraphs 49 and 50 of the FAC ("bait and switch" of plots) as well as paragraphs 59 and 89 of the FAC (unconscionable liquidated damages clause) sufficiently alleged unfair business practices that are within the scope of the UCL. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Lifemark Group Inc.'s motion to strike the prayers for punitive damages, restitution and disgorgement in the first amended complaint is denied. As stated in the ruling on Lifemark's demurrer, plaintiffs have adequately alleged their eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and their tenth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law, which support the prayers that are sought to be stricken by this motion. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2017 Continued To May-24-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Hanlen J. Chang of the law offices of Hanlen J. Chang (415.757.3422) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Tina W. Lu of the law offices of John A. Biard (925.945.4003) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The demurrer having been argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter, the Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendant Lifemark Group Inc.'s motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the complaint is granted with twenty days leave to amend. The complaint lacks sufficient ultimate facts showing that any Lifemark employee acted fraudulently, maliciously or oppressively or that any such conduct could be attributed to Lifemark, a corporation, for purposes of imposing punitive damages liability. If plaintiffs can do so in good faith, they are given leave to amend to correct these deficiencies. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-23-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Hanlen J. Chang of the law offices of Hanlen J. Chang (415.757.3422) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Tina W. Lu of the law offices of John A. Biard (925.945.4003) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court reverses in part and confirms in part the tentative ruling.

Defendant Lifemark Group Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for fraud, eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and the tenth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law as to all signatory plaintiffs and b) OVERRULED as to all ten causes of action as to plaintiffs Kwai Ho Tsang and Lai Fun Tsang, who are minors and not signatories. The complaint lacks sufficient ultimate facts to support the fraud, IIED and UCL claims. The complaint also lacks sufficient ultimate facts to support any claims alleged by Kwai Ho Tsang and Lai Fun Tsang, who are neither signatories to the burial plot contract nor alleged to have any direct dealings with Lifemark. If plaintiffs can do so in good faith, they are given leave to amend to correct these deficiencies. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
